headline: Why Today’s Retirees May Need More Stocks Than Their Parents Did
summary: A comfortable American retirement now runs closer to $70,000 a year than the figures your parents used. Replacing that income from a portfolio is harder than it was a generation ago, because the safe yields that funded the 1990s retiree have collapsed: the national 12-month CD pays under 2%, and the 10-year Treasury sits near ... Why Today’s Retirees May Need More Stocks Than Their Parents Did

headline: Why Paying Off Your Mortgage Early May Be A Retirement Mistake
summary: The standard retirement script says pay off the mortgage before you retire. The logic is simple: eliminate the biggest monthly bill and retirement becomes easier to fund. But today’s interest-rate environment complicates the calculation. A homeowner with a 3% to 4% mortgage may be directing extra cash toward a loan that costs less than the ... Why Paying Off Your Mortgage Early May Be A Retirement Mistake

headline: Testing The Truce
summary: Mixed U.S. stock market updates as yields fall and oil slides on Hormuz traffic rebound. Read more about real estate data here.
